heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Byron Bay
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Byron Bay? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Byron Bay so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Byron rewards families who pick their base carefully. Stay walkable to town and you skip the parking nightmare entirely, or head to Suffolk Park and Broken Head for the quiet beachfront version of Byron most visitors never see.
local_knowledge: Parking is Byron's real challenge, so the smartest move a family can make is staying somewhere you don't need the car. First Sun puts you 500 metres from Main Beach and the town centre. Discovery Parks is the entertainment option with the only waterpark in the area, a short walk from Belongil Beach. Suffolk Park and Broken Head, ten minutes south, are the quiet alternative with patrolled swimming at Broken Head in season and a fraction of the crowds. The lighthouse walk is doable with school-aged kids if you start early. Main Beach is patrolled year-round and stays gentle most days.

if_we_were_booking.label: Walk to town and beach
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose First Sun Holiday Park or Reflections Byron Bay to skip the parking battle entirely and walk to Main Beach, the playground and dinner.

if_we_were_booking.label: Pools and quiet beaches
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Discovery Parks Byron Bay for the waterpark and heated pool, or Suffolk Beachfront and Broken Head if a quieter patrolled beach beats on-site facilities.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Do the Cape Byron lighthouse walk before 8am. You'll beat the heat and the crowds, often spot dolphins from the headland, and be back at the park pool before the day gets busy.

faq.question: Is Byron Bay good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, with the right base. Main Beach is patrolled year-round and gentle, the lighthouse walk suits school-aged kids, and staying walkable to town removes the parking stress that frustrates most visitors.

faq.question: Which Byron Bay holiday park has a pool?
faq.answer: Discovery Parks Byron Bay is the standout, with a waterpark, heated pool and splash pad. The other parks rely on beach access instead.

faq.question: Where should families stay to walk to town?
faq.answer: First Sun Holiday Park sits between Main Beach and the town centre, and Reflections Byron Bay is a short walk from both. Neither requires a car for daily life.

faq.question: Is Suffolk Park a good alternative to staying in Byron?
faq.answer: Yes, especially for families wanting quiet. It's ten minutes south, has its own shops and cafes, and Broken Head's beach is patrolled in season with far fewer people.

faq.question: Where is the safest swimming for young kids?
faq.answer: Main Beach in front of the surf club is patrolled year-round, and Clarkes Beach next to it is usually the calmest stretch. At Suffolk, swim at patrolled Broken Head in season.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities in Byron?
faq.answer: The lighthouse walk, swimming at Main Beach, the beachfront playground near the surf club and watching the sunset from the headland cost nothing.

faq.question: How bad is parking really?
faq.answer: In holiday periods, genuinely difficult from mid-morning. Families staying at walkable parks save an hour a day. If you drive in, arrive before 9am.

faq.question: How far in advance should you book?
faq.answer: Byron's parks are among the most in-demand in the country. School holidays and festival weekends book out many months ahead.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: The cinema in town, the games room at Discovery Parks, and the Macadamia Castle at Knockrow, 15 minutes away, cover most wet days.

faq.question: Is Byron Bay expensive for holiday parks?
faq.answer: Yes, sites and cabins price above nearly everywhere else in NSW. Travelling outside school holidays or staying at Suffolk and Broken Head softens it.
